meta.title: Water Quality & Sewage
meta.subtitle: What did your MP actually do?
meta.description: Storm overflow discharge and water quality regulation in England and Wales. Voting records from UK Parliament. Sewage spill hours are prototype data derived from Environment Agency Annual Return patterns — verify before publishing.
meta.data_note: Action states reflect parliamentary record only. No absence-equals-guilt inference. Scottish and Welsh constituencies operate under devolved water regulation — records reflect Westminster activity only.
meta.geojson_note: Real 2024 Westminster constituency boundaries (July 2024 BFE) are available from the ONS Geography Portal. This prototype uses a tile grid approximation to unblock the demo. Replace grid_col/grid_row with real GeoJSON coordinates for v2.

key_vote.label: Environment Bill — Lords Amendment 45 (Storm Overflow Duty)
key_vote.description: Proposed placing a legal duty on water companies to progressively reduce harm from storm overflow discharges. Defeated 268–272.

sources: Environment Agency Storm Overflow Annual Return — environment.data.gov.uk

acted.label: Active in parliament
acted.description: Voted for and/or publicly advocated stricter water regulation in parliament

raised.label: Raised concern
raised.description: Asked questions or made speeches on water quality. No matching vote found in available data.

opposed.label: Voted against stricter measures
opposed.description: Voted against proposed strengthening of water regulation. This records a vote — it does not imply support for pollution.

no_record.label: No record found
no_record.description: No matching parliamentary activity found in available data. This does not mean the MP has no view on this issue.

constituencies.name: Inverness, Skye and West Ross-shire
constituencies.voted_note: Scottish MPs do not vote on English water regulation
constituencies.sewage_note: Scottish Water — regulated separately from Ofwat under Scottish Parliament

evidence.title: Written question to DEFRA on water quality monitoring

constituencies.fairness_note: Scottish MPs do not vote on English & Welsh water regulation. This shows Westminster record only — Scottish Water is regulated via the Scottish Parliament.

constituencies.name: Aberdeen South
constituencies.voted_note: SNP typically abstains on England-only matters under EVEL conventions
constituencies.sewage_note: Scottish Water — not regulated by Ofwat

evidence.title: Opposition Day debate — Water Quality, England

constituencies.fairness_note: Scottish constituencies use Scottish Water, regulated separately. This record reflects Westminster participation only.

constituencies.name: Edinburgh East and Musselburgh

constituencies.sewage_note: Scottish Water — separate regulator
constituencies.fairness_note: No parliamentary record found in available data for English water quality debates. Scottish MPs represent constituents served by Scottish Water under Scottish Parliament oversight.
